author: phase-4-coordinator
content: Resolved. parse_complex_data now routes both re and im fields through parse_f64_value, which already handles the NaN/Inf string forms (NaN, Inf, Infinity, -Inf, -Infinity) that Python's JSON serializer emits because the JSON grammar has no NaN/Inf literal. Previously the function called as_f64().unwrap() on both fields and panicked on every complex oracle fixture case that contained a non-finite value. 9 new unit tests cover finite, NaN in re, NaN in im, +/-Inf in re, mixed string+number fields, and the single-Object form of the input. Also added 3 tests for the pre-existing parse_f64_value helper that previously had no test coverage. Clippy clean; all tests pass.
